preview_text:
The model rates Netherlands as heavy favourite with a 70% win probability, while Tunisia's chances of victory stand at 9%. This reflects a substantial gap between the two sides according to the statistical forecast.

The draw carries meaningful plausibility at 22%, suggesting the match could remain competitive through regulation. Tunisia's 9% win probability indicates the underdog is not without a path to upset, though the spread between the favourites and challenger is pronounced. A Netherlands victory appears the most likely single outcome by a wide margin.

Model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably. The market prices Netherlands roughly 10 percentage points higher than the model and Tunisia about 2 percentage points lower, while placing less weight on a draw. Polymarket pricing sits even further toward the Netherlands. The dashboard does not claim to know which source better reflects the true match probabilities.
